audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in unbind.c are protected either by `assert(ld != NULL)` (intentional library-contract enforcement; OpenLDAP API treats NULL ld as caller undefined behavior) or by initialization guarantees: `ld->ld_sb` is always non-NULL when `ldap_ld_free` is reached because `ldap_create` (open.c:223-224) aborts to a manual `nomem` handler that never calls `ldap_ld_free` on a partially-initialized handle. `ldap_send_unbind`'s `sb` parameter is gated by `lconn_status == LDAP_CONNST_CONNECTED` in the sole caller (request.c:789), guaranteeing a valid Sockbuf. No network-controlled path exists to force any pointer in unbind.c to NULL.
